subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Power the Addition Arcade by joining parts to make totals within 20.
lesson.concept_explanation: Addition Foundations Within 20 means using a math model, saying what you see, and checking that the answer fits.
lesson.common_misconception: Start with one addend and count on when helpful.
lesson.real_world_connection: Power the Addition Arcade by joining parts to make totals within 20.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use the model and careful counting to solve addition foundations within 20 problems.
lesson.mini_lesson: Use addition model to reason carefully before choosing or writing an answer.

worked_examples.text: Watch the model first, then match it to the answer for addition foundations within 20.
Demo: count, compare, or match each part before answering.

hint_ladder: Say the math words out loud.
Check your answer by doing the action again.

misconception_bank.count_all_error: Start with one addend and count on when helpful.
misconception_bank.missing_addend_confusion: Find the part that makes the whole.
misconception_bank.addend_order_confusion: Addends can switch order and keep the same sum.
misconception_bank.three_addend_tracking_error: Add two numbers first, then add the third.

story.text: Welcome to Addition Arcade Quest. The arcade powers up when you join parts to make totals within 20. Your goal is to solve addition problems with models and math words. You will learn that plus means putting parts together and the total tells how many in all. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson is about addition foundations within 20. Important words are add, plus, equals, total, count on, and model. An addition model shows two parts being joined to make one whole amount. A common mistake is starting the count over when counting on would help, so keep one addend in your head and count on from there. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example. The prompt is 1 plus 2 equals missing number. Step one, read the addition sentence and find the two parts. Step two, build the model with 1 object and 2 more objects. Step three, count all the objects or count on: 1, then 2 more makes 2, 3. The final answer is 3 because 1 plus 2 equals 3. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo shows how addition joins parts. Watch one group combine with another group, then count the total in all. For example, 1 plus 2 means start with 1, add 2 more, and land on 3. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the addition strategy: build both parts, count on, and check the total. The model shows an addition problem, such as 1 plus 2 equals missing number. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question again. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. It may have a larger total within 20 or ask you to count on instead of counting every object from the start. Use the model, keep the first addend in your head, and count on the second addend. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question, and use the hint if you need help.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Show that you can add within 20 and explain the total. Use the model or number line, join the parts, and make sure the answer makes the equation true. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Great work in Addition Arcade Quest. You practiced joining parts, using plus and equals, and finding totals within 20. Those addition strategies help you solve more number stories. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This is your growth profile. It can summarize your attempts, correct answers, and accuracy so you know how your addition practice went. Your next recommendation will help you keep building fact fluency. You can replay the mission or go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
